target.title: fix #84567: [Bug]: Codex bundled harness initialize still hangs in 2026.5.18 isolated cron — surfaces via #64744 timeout-wrapping as 'isolated agent setup timed out before runner start'
target.body:
## Summary

- Bound Codex app-server `initialize` with the remaining startup deadline instead of giving it the full outer timeout after earlier startup work has already elapsed.
- Apply the phase timeout at `client.initialize()` inside the shared/isolated client path, not around the entire factory, so pre-initialize factory hangs still report the generic startup timeout.
- Make the initialize phase timeout fire before the outer startup watchdog, so stalled initialize reports `codex app-server initialize timed out` instead of the generic startup timeout.
- Stop connection-closed retry attempts after the outer startup timeout has already abandoned the startup operation.
- Add regression coverage for the stalled-initialize stdio path, remaining-budget calculation, pre-initialize factory timeout boundary, startup-timeout cleanup, cancellation cleanup, startup RPC timeout behavior, and the real isolated cron `agentTurn` path for `openai/gpt-5.5` via the Codex runtime.

Fixes #84567.

## Root Cause

The Codex startup operation had two timeout layers with mismatched ownership. The outer app-server startup watchdog measured the whole pre-run setup, while the inner shared-client initialize timeout was supposed to guard the first Codex protocol request. Because the inner initialize timeout was created later but still received the full startup timeout duration, both timers could race at the same effective boundary and the outer watchdog could surface the less-specific `codex app-server startup timed out` / cron setup timeout even after the child had already received `initialize`. When the outer timeout closed the client, the background startup attempt could also still treat that connection close as retryable.

## Why this is root cause fix

`initialize` is the first required Codex app-server protocol step before `thread/start`, so the startup invariant is that this phase must either complete or fail inside the same startup deadline. This patch enforces that invariant at the source boundary: startup passes an initialize deadline into the client factory, the shared/isolated client applies the timeout at `client.initialize()`, that phase timer is scheduled to win before the outer watchdog, and the retry loop no longer restarts after the outer startup timeout has already abandoned the attempt. Pre-initialize factory/start-context hangs stay on the generic startup timeout path.

## Why it matters / User impact

Isolated cron users could see `isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` even though the real stall was the Codex app-server `initialize` handshake. That made the failure hard to diagnose and could leave an abandoned startup path racing in the background. With this patch, the user-visible failure points at the stalled Codex initialize phase and cleanup starts before a thread runner is created.

## Fix classification

Root cause fix.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

High for the pre-`thread/start` initialize stall boundary and the issue-shaped isolated cron path: the code now enforces the Codex lifecycle deadline where the protocol can stall, the regression tests cover timer ordering and the real `agentTurn` cron runner route for `openai/gpt-5.5` through the Codex harness, and the real-stdio proof shows the initialize error wins over the generic startup watchdog. The remaining proof gap is a live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron on the reporter's account, which is unavailable in this environment and is disclosed below.

## Maintainer-ready checklist

- **Root cause:** The bug was a timeout-budget invariant mismatch in Codex startup. OpenClaw started the outer app-server startup watchdog before the initialize phase, but the later initialize timeout still received the full startup timeout duration. In the stalled-stdio case, that allowed the generic outer watchdog to win the race even though the real blocked protocol step was `initialize`, and the background startup attempt could still treat the watchdog-induced connection close as retryable.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Codex requires `initialize` before `thread/start`, so the first protocol boundary must complete or fail inside the same startup deadline. This patch derives an initialize deadline from the startup deadline, applies the phase timeout at `client.initialize()` inside shared/isolated client startup, and prevents connection-closed retries once the startup watchdog has already abandoned the attempt. Successful initialize responses and pre-initialize factory hangs still follow their existing paths; only the stalled initialize boundary now reports the correct phase.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Isolated cron users get a precise Codex initialize timeout instead of a generic pre-run setup timeout, making the stalled provider setup diagnosable before any runner starts.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the pre-`thread/start` initialize stall boundary and the issue-shaped isolated cron runner path; the live OAuth account path is not available here and is disclosed in the proof section.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout/retry changes are intentional and bounded: the initialize deadline is only consumed by the shared/isolated `client.initialize()` phase, while the retry guard only applies after the startup watchdog has already fired. The broad catch in the cron proof only aborts and drains the in-flight test run if the expected initialize request is never written, so it prevents test leakage rather than changing product behavior.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not change live OAuth auth selection, model routing, successful Codex app-server startup behavior, post-`thread/start` turn execution, user config, schema, protocol payloads, or public APIs.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is the Codex app-server lifecycle contract: `initialize` must complete before `thread/start`, and OpenClaw now enforces that contract at the client lifecycle boundary instead of post-processing a downstream error string. OpenAI Codex auth profile compatibility stays on the canonical `openai` provider routing boundary.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts` and `src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression suite locks the stalled real-stdio initialize boundary, the pre-initialize generic startup-timeout boundary, the abandoned-startup cleanup path, and the real isolated cron `agentTurn` route for `openai/gpt-5.5` with `openai-codex:default`, including both initialize-timeout attribution and successful initialize reaching `thread/start`.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The new cron proof uses the real isolated cron runner and a Codex app-server protocol harness instead of a live provider account, so it exercises the issue-specific runner/model/profile/thread-start boundary deterministically without requiring reporter-only OAuth credentials.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** The current `status: ⏳ waiting on author` label is addressed by this update: the author-actionable ClawSweeper finding is fixed below, proof remains supplied/sufficient, and the PR body maps the changed boundary and fresh verification.
- **RF-002:** The factory-wide initialize wrapper was removed. `attempt-startup.ts` no longer wraps the whole `attemptClientFactory` promise with `codex app-server initialize timed out`.
- **RF-003:** Pre-initialize startup work can no longer be reported as an initialize protocol stall by the new patch. Startup passes an initialize deadline through the factory options, and `shared-client.ts` consumes it only around `client.initialize()`.
- **RF-004:** Focused `run-attempt` coverage was run on this branch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever"` passed.
- **RF-005:** The repair keeps the remaining-budget initialize fix while restoring the generic startup-timeout behavior for pre-initialize factory hangs.
- **RF-006:** The pre-initialize factory-hang path is covered by the focused `run-attempt` regression and remains on `codex app-server startup timed out`.
- **RF-007:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts` passed: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.
- **RF-008:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever"` passed: 1 test passed / 85 skipped.
- **RF-009:** `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/client-factory.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts` passed with no lint findings.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review after this proof update; remote checks should run on the pushed head.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

No known local author action remains after this update. Maintainers still need to decide whether the deterministic local isolated cron proof plus the real-stdio stalled initialize proof are sufficient, or whether reporter-account live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron validation is required. That live reporter OAuth environment is not available here.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- **RF-001:** maintainer-decision needed. This update keeps the live OAuth limitation disclosed and adds deterministic isolated cron `agentTurn` proof, so maintainers can decide whether the remaining reporter-account live OAuth gap is acceptable.
- **RF-002:** fixed/disclosed. The PR body explains the intentional timeout/retry ordering change and the risk boundary: stalled `initialize` now fails before the outer startup watchdog, and connection-closed retries stop only after the watchdog has abandoned startup.
- **RF-003:** fixed as far as local proof allows; external proof unavailable. This update adds the real isolated cron runner path for `agentTurn`, `openai/gpt-5.5`, and the `openai-codex:default` profile id, but it still does not claim a live reporter OpenAI OAuth account run.

## Codex contract checked

Checked sibling `../codex` source before changing the Codex boundary:

- `codex-rs/app-server/README.md:76` and `codex-rs/app-server/README.md:85` document that clients must send `initialize` before any other method and then send `initialized`.
- `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs:435` through `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs:449` define the `initialize` and `thread/start` client requests as distinct protocol steps.
- `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:59` through `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:61` reject repeated initialize calls, and `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:148` through `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:153` send the initialize response before marking outbound initialized.
- `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/thread_processor.rs:377` through `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/thread_processor.rs:394` handle `thread/start` after initialization as the next lifecycle phase.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** stalled real stdio Codex initialize is bounded before `thread/start` and reports the initialize phase error.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, OpenClaw worktree after this patch, with a real stdio child process spawned through the Codex app-server client startup path.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** copied output from the real-stdio proof:

```text
Behavior addressed: stalled real stdio Codex initialize is bounded before thread/start.
Real environment tested: Linux Node v22.22.0; spawned /usr/local/node/bin/node <evidence>/stalled-codex-app-server.mjs
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm exec tsx evidence/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts
Evidence after fix: observed error "codex app-server initialize timed out" after 9855 ms; onStartupTimeoutCalls=0; copied stub log lines are included below
Observed result after fix: initialize phase error won; generic codex app-server startup timed out was not surfaced.
What was not tested: a live OpenAI OAuth account or real Codex binary responding successfully after initialize.
```

Copied stub log from the real stdio child:

```text
2026-06-02T15:13:51.604Z stub-start pid=<redacted>
2026-06-02T15:13:51.609Z received method=initialize id=1
2026-06-02T15:14:00.757Z stub-exit signal=stdin-end
```

- **Observed result after fix:** the stdio child received the real `initialize` request, did not answer, OpenClaw raised `codex app-server initialize timed out`, and the generic `codex app-server startup timed out` message did not win.
- **What was not tested:** a live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron on the reporter's account. This environment does not have that OAuth profile, so the live proof uses a deterministic real-stdio stalled app-server child to exercise the same pre-`thread/start` initialize boundary without external credentials.

Additional isolated cron proof:

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** the real isolated cron `agentTurn` runner path for `openai/gpt-5.5` selects the Codex harness, preserves the user-selected `openai-codex:default` auth profile id, attributes an initialize stall to the Codex app-server initialize phase, and reaches `thread/start` after a successful initialize response.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node/Vitest cron test runner, OpenClaw worktree after this patch, real `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn` execution path, and an in-memory Codex app-server protocol harness. The auth fixture uses a token-backed local credential for the `openai-codex:default` profile id with canonical provider `openai`; it does not claim a live reporter OAuth refresh.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts --testTimeout 60000
```

- **Evidence after fix:** copied output from the isolated cron proof validation:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 [local path redacted]


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Start at  03:30:05
   Duration  22.49s (transform 9.40s, setup 430ms, import 15.17s, tests 6.59s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** the stalled-initialize cron test observed `initialize`, returned `codex app-server initialize timed out`, did not surface `isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` or `codex app-server startup timed out`, recorded the diagnostic under `agent-run` rather than `cron-setup`, and did not send `thread/start`. The successful-initialize cron test observed `thread/start`, responded with a schema-shaped Codex thread/start result, and the isolated cron run resolved with `status: "ok"`.
- **What was not tested:** a live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron on the reporter's account or a real Codex binary completing a live provider turn.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever"`
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts --testTimeout 60000`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/client-factory.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts`

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever"`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 1 test passed / 85 skipped.
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts --testTimeout 60000`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 2 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/client-factory.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
  - Result: no lint findings.
- `pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts`
  - Result: copied in **Real behavior proof** above.

## Patch quality notes

- **Timeout/retry change:** This intentionally changes timeout ordering, not the configured timeout amount. Startup passes an initialize deadline with a 150ms head start so the shared/isolated `client.initialize()` timeout can win before the outer watchdog. The `2_000ms` value in `attempt-startup.test.ts` is only a test cleanup wait, not a production timeout.
- **Factory boundary:** The phase timeout is no longer wrapped around the entire `attemptClientFactory` promise. Pre-initialize factory/start-context hangs still use the generic `codex app-server startup timed out` path, covered by the focused `run-attempt` regression.
- **Runtime contract boundary:** This is not a post-processing or mirror/projection transformation. The change carries a startup deadline into the Codex app-server client lifecycle and consumes it only at the first protocol request, `client.initialize()`, before `thread/start` can be sent.
- **Compatibility / public contract:** No user config, schema, protocol payload, or public app-server API changes. The existing PR already added optional internal factory timeout wiring; callers that omit it keep the previous default behavior. This patch changes the Codex startup caller to pass an initialize deadline and shared/isolated client startup to consume it only at `client.initialize()`.
- **Related open PR scan:** `init-pr` and `check-upstream-fixed` checked the existing PR against current `origin/main`; main does not already cover this repair, and there is no overlap between current main changes and this PR's changed files. No broader canonical open PR was identified for this private Codex startup timeout boundary.
- **Out of scope:** This does not change live OAuth auth selection, model routing, successful Codex app-server startup behavior, or post-`thread/start` runtime behavior.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `availability`, `compatibility`.
- **Risk explanation:** Availability risk is limited to Codex app-server startup: a stalled initialize now fails earlier with `codex app-server initialize timed out` inside the already configured startup budget. Compatibility risk is limited to the optional factory timeout path already present on this PR; there is no new config/schema/protocol surface.
- **Why acceptable:** The change narrows an ambiguous startup timeout to the required first protocol phase and prevents retries only after the outer startup watchdog has already abandoned the attempt. It does not introduce a fallback, broaden retries, or change successful startup behavior.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
